#  Who can be designated as an 'Assignee' on an RFI? 
##  Answer 
For a user to be designated as an 'Assignee' on an RFI, the following must be true:
  * The user be added the Project level Directory tool.
  * The user must be granted 'Standard' level permissions or higher on the project's RFIs tool.


To designate a user as an 'Assignee' on an RFI while creating or editing the RFI, you must have 'Admin' level permissions on the RFIs tool. When a 'Draft' RFI is placed into the 'Open' status, the system will automatically shift the [Ball In Court ](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms)responsibility to the RFIs 'Assignees'. An RFI's 'Assignee' can add other 'Assignees' to the RFI when they have the current [Ball In Court ](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms)responsibility.
To learn how to respond to an RFI as an assignee, see [Respond to an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/respond-to-an-rfi).
